title: MSA redline: regional batch sessions & calendar slots
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Park <sophie.park@clearsky.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MSA redline: regional batch sessions & calendar slots

Hi Sophie,

Thanks for sending the redlines over earlier. To avoid repeated one-off calls and speed up resolution, wed like to propose three short regional batch sessions during which our legal and procurement teams will walk the redlines with your counterparts. The goal is to triage items we can resolve live and surface a small set of blockers for offline follow-up.

Suggested 60-minute windows (we included a provisional .ics):
- APAC: Tue, 7 Sep 2:00 UTC (08:00 / 10:00 / 14:00 SGT options in the .ics)
- EMEA: Wed, 8 Sep 2:00 UTC (09:00 / 12:00 / 16:00 BST options)
- Americas: Thu, 9 Sep 2:00 UTC (09:00 / 11:00 / 15:00 PDT options)

Can you confirm one preferred slot per region and the attendees (legal + procurement) from Clearsky? If Americas needs a slightly shorter slot for your legal team we can run that one as a 50-minute meeting.

I attached a compiled PDF of the redlines you shared and the calendar stub. If it helps, Laura (CC) will coordinate the invites from our side.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachments: Clearsky_MSA_v3_redlines.pdf, regional_batch_slots.ics

From: Sophie Park <sophie.park@clearsky.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Mark Dwyer <m.dwyer@clearsky.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Aug 2027 17:47:00 +0100
Subject: Re: MSA redline: regional batch sessions & calendar slots

Ben,

Batch sessions sound efficient and appreciated. Quick responses from our side:
- Ive added our procurement lead Mark Dwyer to the thread (cc). Hell own scheduling on our end.
- APAC: 10:00 SGT looks best for our Singapore/HK teams.
- EMEA: 12:00 BST works.
- Americas: 11:00 PDT is OK but wed ask you to end at :50 to allow our counsel to hop to an existing call.

Were preparing a lightly redlined PDF and will upload that; we prefer DocuSign for final signature routing rather than back-and-forth PDFs. Please confirm who from Redwood legal will be on each session.

Thanks,
Sophie Park
Head of Procurement | Clearsky Analytics
sophie.park@clearsky.com

Attachment: Clearsky_MSA_v3_redlines.pdf (incoming)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Park <sophie.park@clearsky.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Mark Dwyer <m.dwyer@clearsky.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MSA redline: regional batch sessions & calendar slots

Sophie / Mark,

Appreciate the quick confirmation. Well lock APAC at 10:00 SGT, EMEA at 12:00 BST, and Americas at 11:00 PDT (Americas will be 50 minutes). Laura will send invites from redwood.com with a shared agenda.

Redwood legal attendees: Hannah Schmitt (Head of Contracts), Evelyn Hart (Contracts Associate). They will run the walk-through and flag any clauses that need legal-level review. If there are specific reviewers on your side we should expect legal to be familiar with, please list them.

Weve also attached a short pre-review doc (legal_preflight_notes.docx) that our team will use to speed the sessions 5 items weve already marked as low-risk / clarification-only.

Ben

Attachments: legal_preflight_notes.docx

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Park <sophie.park@clearsky.com>, Mark Dwyer <m.dwyer@clearsky.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 31 Aug 2027 08:22:00 -0400
Subject: Introductions + what to expect on the batch calls (Clearsky MSA)

Hi Sophie / Mark,

Quick intro 5 Hannah here from Redwood Legal. Ill be on the calls listed below with Evelyn. A few notes so we can hit the ground running: 
- Well open each session with a 5-minute agenda review, then go clause-by-clause for 40 minutes, and use the last 55 to capture action items.
- Please have a single point of contact who can commit to clarifying procurement or customer terms live; it dramatically speeds closure.
- If there are any commercial exceptions (credits, SLA changes), flag those up front so we can loop in finance/ops async.

Ive attached our 1-page preflight so you can see the low-risk items we suggest resolving in-session. If you prefer, we can pre-mark the PDF with comments prior to the call.

See you on the call,
Hannah Schmitt
Head of Contracts | Redwood Inference
hannah.schmitt@redwood.com

Attachment: legal_preflight_notes.docx

From: Sophie Park <sophie.park@clearsky.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Cc: Mark Dwyer <m.dwyer@clearsky.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 1 Sep 2027 10:00:00 +0100
Subject: Re: MSA redline: regional batch sessions & calendar slots

Thanks everyone 5 that all works for Clearsky. Mark will join as primary procurement lead; our legal will be Jane Ortiz (j.ortiz@clearsky.com) for APAC/EMEA and Raj Patel (r.patel@clearsky.com) for Americas.

I confirmed the three invites in the .ics and uploaded our lightly redlined PDF to the shared drive (link below). We confirm DocuSign for final signature; Mark will send the account contact for routing once the legal clean copy is agreed.

DocuSign placeholder: https://docusign.com/placeholder/clearsky-msav3
Shared drive: https://drive.google.com/clearsky-msav3

Appreciate the structured approach 5 see you in the sessions.

Best,
Sophie

Attachments: Clearsky_MSA_v3_redlines.pdf

title: Q3 readout: scoring packet + rollforward narrative (draft)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Cho <sara.cho@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Q3 readout: scoring packet + rollforward narrative (draft)

Team —

Attached is the working packet for the Q3 leadership readout: Q3_OKR_scores_v2.xlsx and the heatmap PDF. I ran a fresh pass on the objective-level scores and drafted short narratives for the exec slides. High level asks:

- Please review your objective scores and the single-line verdict in column F. I flagged any item where the score changed more than 0.5 from the midquarter assessment.
- Add one concrete evidence link under the Evidence column for each objective you own (Drive links or Jira tickets).
- Call out any unresolved disagreements directly in reply so I can surface them in Monday's prep.

Scoring note (for transparency): we used a 0-1.0 scale mapped to 0-100 for the slides. The guidance I used: 0.0-0.39 = Miss, 0.4-0.69 = Partial, 0.7-0.89 = Mostly, 0.9-1.0 = Achieved. I also included a short risk sentence per objective — please make those sharper if needed.

Quick examples from the sheet:
- Objective: "Serving latency at p95 < 250ms" — current score 0.72 (Mostly). Evidence: latency dashboard link. Risk: traffic spike sensitivity for long-tail tails.
- Objective: "Reduce token cost per 1k requests by 20%" — current score 0.55 (Partial). Evidence: Optimize experiments listed; outstanding rollout on model-quantization.

I'll compile final version after comments. Plan: present to execs on Thursday (internal dry-run Wednesday). If you have conflicts with those times, flag them.

Ben
Ben Carter
Director, Product Operations
Redwood Inference
Attachments: Q3_OKR_scores_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet), okr-heatmap-q3.pdf (visual), evidence-pack-q3.zip (supporting evidence)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Cho <sara.cho@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 12:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q3 readout: scoring packet + rollforward narrative (draft)

Thanks Ben — quick pass from my side. A couple of small edits inline in the sheet and one substantive change:

- Objective: Developer onboarding time — I think 0.9 is optimistic given the metrics; propose 0.78 (Mostly). I added the measurement links in Evidence.
- For the "Reduce token cost" objective, I've annotated that the rollout to dedicated customers is still gated by infra work (ticket INF-1123) which pushes completion into early Q4. I suggested we mark the narrative as "requires follow-through" rather than "on track."

Also added a short note recommending we replace the one-line risk for Latency with the p99 delta (we have a concrete number in tracing).

On the slides: can we move the heatmap slide earlier (after the score summary) so it frames the conversation?

Marissa
--
Marissa Cole
Sr. PM, Developer Experience
Redwood Inference

On Mon, Aug 30, 2027 at 10:12 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Attached is the working packet for the Q3 leadership readout...


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Cho <sara.cho@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q3 readout: scoring packet + rollforward narrative (draft)

Thanks — solid work. Two strategic asks from my side before we lock:

1) For Objective "Model catalog stability" (score 0.62), add a short paragraph on customer impact: include two customer tickets (ACME-2345 and SAGE-998) showing regression frequency. That context will help the execs see why this is prioritized.

2) For the Rollforward slide: include a one-line mitigation plan that maps to a single owner and a target date (e.g., "Infra: INF-1123 -> Nov 12"). Otherwise the execs will ask who is driving the fix.

If Naomi can attach the evidence pack and confirm the ACME tickets, I'm happy to sign off on the compiled slides.

Vivek
VP, Product

On Mon, Aug 30, 2027 at 12:05 PM Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks Ben — quick pass from my side....


From: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Cho <sara.cho@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Tue, 31 Aug 2027 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q3 readout: scoring packet + rollforward narrative (draft)

All — attaching the evidence pack (evidence-pack-q3.zip) and confirming the two ACME tickets Vivek referenced: ACME-2345, SAGE-998. I dropped a small folder inside the Drive packet with the tracing snippets for p99/p95 deltas and the infra ticket INF-1123 link.

A couple of clarifications I added to the sheet:
- Developer onboarding: added the cohort analysis showing median time dropped 14% vs baseline (supports 0.78).
- Token cost: flagged that the quantization playbook reduced unit cost in staging but introduces a 1.5% accuracy delta on rare prompts — note included under "tradeoffs."

Attachments: evidence-pack-q3.zip (contains the cited tracing snippets and ticket links).

Naomi
Manager, Data & Observability

On Mon, Aug 30, 2027 at 3:20 PM Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks — solid work. Two strategic asks...


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Cho <sara.cho@acme-analytics.com>
Date: Tue, 31 Aug 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Finalized Q3 packet and next steps (for Wednesday dry-run)

Thanks everyone — I compiled the final packet with the changes above. Summary of decisions:

- Developer onboarding: settled at 0.78 (Mostly). Evidence links are in the sheet (Marissa, thanks).
- Model catalog stability: 0.62 (Partial). Naomi confirmed ACME-2345 and SAGE-998 in evidence pack.
- Token cost objective: 0.55 (Partial). We call out INF-1123 and committed to a rollforward owner (Infra team, owner: Priya N.).

Action list (short):
- Marissa: reorder the slides so heatmap is slide 3; update the speaker notes (by 09:00 Wed).
- Naomi: upload the Drive folder link to the deck and unzip the evidence pack into the shared folder (done).
- Vivek: prepare the one-line mitigation/owner bullets for the rollforward slide (done).
- Ben: run the Wednesday dry-run 11:00-11:45 PT; will share the calendar invite.

I'll post the final readout link here in the thread after I push the slides to Drive. If anything critical comes up overnight, reply-all and I'll fold it in.

Ben
Director, Product Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachments: final deck uploaded to https://drive.google.com/open?id=1gQ3_example_scores

title: Re: Data Processing Annex - subprocessor inventory & evidence
preview:
From: Lisa Chen <lisa.chen@acmehealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: privacy@acmehealth.com
Date: Sun, 29 Aug 2027 09:12:03 -0700
Subject: Data Processing Annex - initial evidence & subprocessor list

Hi Aisha / Marcus,

Thanks again for the demo last week. As part of our procurement checklist we need to close out the Data Processing Annex before legal signs off. Can you please: 

- Share a completed subprocessor inventory (name, role, country, contract status)
- Provide the latest SOC 2 summary / cover letter and indicate whether any carve-outs apply
- Confirm the mechanism you propose for EU -> US transfers (SCCs, DPF, or other) and whether Redwood will sign a short EU Addendum if required

We have a hard deadline to present to counsel by EOD Thursday (9/2). I attached our template DPA to this thread — we've highlighted a few items up front in the document (liability, retention).

Thanks,
Lisa
Lisa Chen
Head of Privacy & Compliance
Acme Health

Attachments: Acme-DPA-template.pdf


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Chen <lisa.chen@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 29 Aug 2027 11:04:15 -0700
Subject: Re: Data Processing Annex - initial evidence & subprocessor list

Hi Lisa —

Appreciate the clear asks. Quick update and proposed next steps: 

1) Subprocessor inventory: attaching a CSV export with current subprocessors and locations. This includes the runtime/CDN/storage providers relevant to hosted deployments and our Dedicated/private integrations.
2) SOC 2: attached is the SOC 2 summary report cover (Q2 2027). Full report is available under NDA — happy to arrange secure transfer if counsel prefers.
3) Transfer mechanism: Redwood supports SCCA/SCCs for EU transfers today; we are in parallel testing Data Privacy Framework flows for some customers but cannot rely on that unilaterally yet. For this engagement we propose: SCCs + a one-page EU Addendum to address specific Acme requirements (data residency for backups, subprocessors list notice window).

I am attaching our initial redlines to your template (DPA-AcmeHealth-redlines-v1.pdf). Grace has reviewed and added counsel notes inline (see her email below).

Proposed timeline:
- EOD Mon (8/30): Redwood to provide the inventory + SOC2 summary + redlines (done, attached)

From: Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Chen <lisa.chen@acmehealth.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 08:22:40 -0700
Subject: Re: Data Processing Annex - initial evidence & subprocessor list (privacy counsel comments)

Hi Lisa / Aisha,

Thanks — I reviewed the Acme template and Aisha's redlines. High-level counsel comments below (inline redlines in PDF):

1) Transfers: We will implement SCCs as the lawful mechanism. I propose a short EU Addendum that:
   - Declares SCCs as the default transfer mechanism
   - Confirms Redwood's commitments on backup residency and responsible subprocessors for EU customer data
   - Includes a 30-day notice period for adding subprocessors with a material change threshold (security or location).

2) Subprocessor inventory: The CSV is complete for our primary hosted stack, but note: the inventory flags two categories — "managed partners" (used by Dedicated deployments) and "transient vendors" (cache/CDN providers that we rely on across regions). For the latter, we provide control evidence rather than per-tenant contracts. If Acme requires per-subprocessor contracts we can discuss carveouts for Dedicated deployment only.

3) Liability & caps: Your template requests unlimited liability for certain data incidents. Our position is to keep a contractual cap tied to fees (standard market practice). I'm proposing a compromise: a raised liability cap specifically for privacy breaches tied to a multiple of annual fees for the first 12 months after breach discovery. Details in PDF.

4) Retention & deletion: We need clearer language on what Acme expects for backups and cold archives. Redwood's hosted offering maintains encrypted backups across regions for 90 days by default; Dedicated customers can request custom retention. Suggest we add a short schedule to Annex A for retention defaults and options.

5) Evidence handoff: SOC2 summary attached. For deeper evidence (control narratives, penetration testing artifacts) we will provide access via a secure drive link or NDA-protected exchange. Please confirm which route counsel prefers.

Next steps I suggest: a brief 30-minute call with Acme counsel to confirm (a) transfer mechanism + addendum scope, and (b) whether per-subprocessor contracts are required for their procurement team. Aisha / Marcus — can you propose 30m slots Tues 08/31 10–12 PT or Wed 09/01 13–16 PT?

Best regards,
Grace Oconnor
Privacy Counsel, Redwood Inference

title: Post-call synthesis: HealthCrest bake-off priorities & interop probes
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@healthcrest.com>, Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@healthbridge.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call synthesis: HealthCrest bake-off priorities & interop probes

Hi Elena / Tom —

Thanks again for a productive call this morning. Quick synthesis of what we heard and a proposed set of follow-up experiments so we can demonstrate parity vs your incumbents and unblock procurement. My goal here is to make the next week concrete.

Key pain points you reiterated
- Unreliable inference tail latency during peak claims ingestion (spikes > 1s unacceptable)
- Token-level cost is ballooning with current batching strategy; unpredictable monthly spend
- Difficulty integrating vendor inference with on-prem compliance (KMS + VPC)

Primary goals for bake-off
1) 95th/99th latency under 250ms for streaming/short-turn responses (<= 512 tokens)
2) Stable unit cost projection for 100k monthly active requests with mixed-length payloads
3) Clear plan for private deployment (VPC + KMS) and audit log export

Decision criteria (what your committee will score)
- Latency SLA (weight 35%)
- Cost predictability & unit economics (weight 25%)
- Integration/security fit (weight 20%)
- Support/operational tooling (weight 20%)

Open questions we captured
- Can you provide a representative 48-hour sample of traffic (sanitized)?
- Are there firm constraints on peak concurrency or GPU SKU availability for the POC?
- Is your procurement team able to accept a short-term reserved commitment for a dedicated pool?

Proposed follow-up tests (owner / target)
- Test A: Prefix/KV cache simulation with your traffic profile (Redwood / Karthik) — run by 2027-09-03
- Test B: Quantized 4-bit parity (speed vs quality) vs current provider (Redwood / Logan) — run by 2027-09-05
- Test C: Private deployment smoke (VPC + KMS integration) with audit log capture (Customer + Redwood infra) — kickoff 2027-09-06

Attachments I will circulate after this thread: interop-test-plan.xlsx (detailed test steps) and a short Redwood vs incumbent feature checklist.

Next immediate asks from you
- Elena: share a sanitized 48-hour traffic dump or a small synthetic payload set by EOD Mon 2027-08-30
- Procurement: confirm if a 30-day reserved test is acceptable (we can offer credit if you want flexibility)

If that aligns, I’ll lock calendar invites for the technical working session (45 min) and a decision readout for 2027-09-09.

Thanks —
Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0124

Attachment stubs: interop-test-plan.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet), redwood-vs-competitor-shortlist.pdf (application/pdf)

From: Elena Park <elena.park@healthcrest.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@healthbridge.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Aug 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call synthesis: HealthCrest bake-off priorities & interop probes

Camila —

Appreciate the clear recap. Quick clarifications from our side:
- We can provide a sanitized 48-hour payload by Monday morning (I'll remove PHI). Expect ~120k requests total, ~60% < 256 tokens, 30% 256-1024, 10% longer.
- On procurement: short-term reserved (30 days) is likely acceptable if your team can guarantee no surprise overage billing during the test window.
- For private deployment: our infra team will need HSM/KMS integration details and an audit log schema. Can you share a sample log format?

One more priority we discussed on the call that I want to emphasize: result determinism on high-sensitivity claims classification. If we run the same short prompt repeatedly during load, we need the output to be stable within the tolerance already established in our baseline tests.

I can attend the technical working session next Thursday (09-02) after 10am PT. Tom will loop in if he has additional commercial questions.

Thanks —
Elena Park
Director, Applied Automation | HealthCrest Network
elena.park@healthcrest.com

On Fri, 27 Aug 2027 at 09:12 AM Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Elena / Tom —
> 
> Thanks again for a productive call this morning. Quick synthesis of what we heard and a proposed set of follow-up experiments so we can demonstrate parity vs your incumbents and unblock procurement. My goal here is to make the next week concrete.
> 
> [snip original message for thread brevity]


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Aug 2027 14:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call synthesis: HealthCrest bake-off priorities & interop probes (technical plan)

Camila / Team —

Draft technical plan (summary) based on call notes and Elena's reply. I attached the detailed workbook (interop-test-plan.xlsx) with step-by-step scripts and expected metrics. High-level:

1) Data intake & workload replay - Need: sanitized 48-hour payload from HealthCrest (JSONL) + sample headers for auth/tenant-id. - Method: continuous replay at scaled concurrency to preserve real inter-arrival times.

2) Latency SLO tests - Measure p50/p95/p99 for streaming & non-streaming routes for 512-token and 1024-token scenarios. - Environment: Redwood hosted vs Redwood Dedicated (single 8xA100 pool) vs incumbent.

3) Caching/Prefix tests - Inject repeated prefixes (claims templates) to validate KV cache hit behavior and tail smoothing.

4) Quantization and quality parity - 4-bit and 8-bit quant runs against reference prompts. Evaluate with automated ROUGE / domain-specific classification F1.

5) Private deployment smoke - Quick VPC handshake, KMS key exchange (we need your KMS contact), audit log stream to S3-equivalent (we will provide schema).

Resource asks
- HealthCrest: JSONL sample and a contact for KMS/HSM integration (name + infra email)
- Redwood: request to reserve one 8xA100 for Sep 3–7 window. Marissa, can you confirm procurement for short reserved pool credits?

Estimated timeline (conservative)
- Sep 1: Data ingest + environment prep
- Sep 3–5: Latency, caching, quant runs
- Sep 6: Private deployment smoke + audit log validation
- Sep 7: Internal score synthesis and readout prep

If everyone agrees I will push the workbook to the Drive link and populate the scripts by EOD tomorrow.

Karthik Iyer
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com

Attachment: interop-test-plan.xlsx (spreadsheet with scripts and dashboards)

From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Elena Park <elena.park@healthcrest.com>, Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@healthbridge.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 27 Aug 2027 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call synthesis: HealthCrest bake-off priorities & interop probes

Elena / Tom —

Perfect — thanks for the quick turnaround on the sample. We're aligned on the runbook and timing. Next concrete steps from here:
- Elena: please drop the sanitized JSONL to the Drive folder linked below by Mon 08-30; Karthik will confirm receipt and run an initial smoke.
- Redwood: Karthik/Logan will reserve the 8xA100 pool and publish test invites; Marissa will confirm credits for a short reserved window.
- Schedule: technical working session Thurs 09-02 @ 11:00am PT (45 min) — I will send the invite; decision readout 09-09.

Link to shared materials: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-healthcrest-bakeoff
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/HC-2027-045

If anything changes on your side, send a brief note here and we can adjust. Looking forward to the tests — we should be able to demonstrate the latency and cost delta quickly.

Best —
Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0124

title: Summit AI - performance targets, trade-offs & immediate next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Kim <alex.kim@summitai.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Aug 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Summit AI - performance targets, trade-offs & immediate next steps

Alex — thanks again for the call earlier. Quick written recap so we have alignment and clear next steps from the product + infra perspective. I kept this intentionally tight.

Summary of what we heard (from Summit AI):
- Primary workload: real-time summarization + follow-up Q&A inside your agent console (streaming tokens to UI).
- Target latency: P95 end-to-end under 300ms for short summaries (~200 token outputs).
- Concurrency/throughput: expected 350 concurrent users peak with bursts to ~1,200 qps for short requests.
- Streaming: required; partial tokens should appear within ~120ms of start and then stream increments.
- Accuracy bar: highest priority for summaries; willing to accept small model-quality tradeoffs for ~2x cost savings but not for critical flows.

Preliminary cost/perf levers we discussed (how Redwood typically hits these SLOs):
1) Continuous batching: keep a small batching window (5–10ms) for short requests to improve GPU utilization without adding material latency.
2) KV/prefix caching: cache recent context embeddings and KV caches for repeated assistant prompts to cut repeated compute.
3) Model variants: production path uses a higher-capacity model for fallback but serves most traffic on a quantized medium model.
4) Quantization profiles: 4-bit quantization for the medium model yields big cost wins; we keep a higher-precision variant for the top X% of flows.
5) Dynamic routing: route latency-sensitive burst traffic to reserved capacity and lower-cost requests to pooled capacity.

Immediate asks / next steps I propose:
- Ben to send a proposed MVP config (model + quantization + batching + cache policy) and rough monthly cost estimate. (action: Ben — by EOD Thu 8/26)
- Summit to confirm peak qps distribution (burst patterns vs sustained). If you can share a short sample traffic trace that would be ideal. (action: Alex/Priya)
- Sync: 30-minute tech follow-up to review the proposed config and run a brief perf projection. (action: everyone — propose times)

Attachment: summit_perf_grid.xlsx — quick matrix mapping model variants, quantization, expected p95, and cost-per-1M tokens.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0112


From: Alex Kim <alex.kim@summitai.com>

Cc: Priya Rao <priya.rao@summitai.com>
Date: Wed, 25 Aug 2027 16:08:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Summit AI - performance targets, trade-offs & immediate next steps

Ben — thanks for the concise recap, helpful. A few clarifications from our side:
- The 350 concurrent users is sustained on business hours; the 1,200 qps are 1–2 minute bursts when customers open live sessions (bursty but short).
- For streaming, we can accept a ~30ms jitter on the first packet, but overall P95 must stay near your 300ms target.
- Regarding accuracy: we run a small eval set — I'll send results from yesterday (baseline and acceptable delta).

Questions:
1) In your grid, what trade-offs do you typically see between 4-bit quantized medium model vs 8-bit quantized small model on p95 latency for streaming? (we care about smooth streaming behavior)
2) Is continuous batching compatible with our expected burst profile (short, intense bursts)? Are there knob/guardrails to avoid increased tail latency during bursts?

I'll attach the brief requirements doc (summit_requirements.pdf) and a one-hour sample traffic trace for last week. We'll upload the trace to the shared folder as well.

Available for the follow-up call tomorrow between 9:00–11:00 PT.

—Alex
Alex Kim
Head of AI Platform, Summit AI
alex.kim@summitai.com | +1 617-555-0223


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Alex Kim <alex.kim@summitai.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Aug 2027 08:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Summit AI - performance targets, trade-offs & immediate next steps

Looping in Marissa for capacity/ops estimates. A few internal notes and a recommended approach for the tech follow-up:
- Approach: propose an incremental MVP that meets streaming p95 <= 300ms for 90% of traffic on day one, with a defined escalation path for the top-10% highest-accuracy flows. That keeps cost bounded while safeguarding your accuracy-critical flows.
- MVP config to present:
  * Model path: serve majority traffic on MQ-3 (quantized 4-bit medium) with a higher-precision HQ-1 model for canary/fallback (automatic routing).
  * Batching: dynamic micro-batching with 8ms base window + burst override (burst override drops to 1ms when queue length exceeds threshold).
  * Caching: 60s TTL for prefix cache on conversational sessions; warm-up policy for first 10 requests.
  * Reserved capacity plan: reserve a small pool (2 x xlarge GPU) to accept burst anchors during peak 1–2 minute windows.
- Cost estimate: Marissa, can you produce a 3-scenario cost estimate (conservative / expected / aggressive) for 1M, 5M, 20M monthly tokens using the attached grid as baseline? We should include delta for HQ-1 fallback usage at 5% traffic.

Ben — if you can draft the MVP config and a short slide with perf expectations (p95/p99, token cost) before the tech call, we can run numbers live.

Thanks — Lauren
Lauren Bishop
Director, Product (Observability & Ops), Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Kim <alex.kim@summitai.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Priya Rao <priya.rao@summitai.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Aug 2027 09:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Summit AI - performance targets, trade-offs & immediate next steps

Alex / Priya — thanks. I'll draft the MVP config + 3-scenario cost sheet and share a short deck by 5pm PT today. Proposed tech follow-up: Thu 8/26 at 10:00 AM PT (30 minutes) — drop me preferred calendar link and I'll send an invite.

Quick answers to your earlier Qs:
- 4-bit medium generally gives ~1.8–2.5x cost reduction vs 8-bit small for similar p95 in short streaming flows because the medium model requires fewer attention passes; we show better streaming smoothness when paired with continuous batching + short micro-windows.
- Continuous batching: yes — we add a burst override (sub-ms window) and buffer-shed policy to prevent queue growth during intense spikes. That keeps the tail bounded.

I'll attach the MVP deck and cost grid to the invite.

Ben
Ben Carter
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
Attachments: summit_perf_grid.xlsx

Elena —

Quick weekly check-in on the MAP and trial milestones. Attached is the working tracker (finera_map_v2.xlsx) and a one-page acceptance matrix (eval_acceptance_matrix.pdf). Highlights:

- Current milestone on data ingest test (Milestone 2) is delayed ~3 business days due to S3 permission mismatch we discovered last week.
- We still expect the evaluation run (Milestone 3) to start the week of Aug 30 if the ingest is cleared.
- Acceptance criteria tab in the matrix has two items flagged: latency under 250ms for 95th pct and tokenization parity for the custom tokenizer.

Action items proposed this week (owners added in the tracker):
1) Finera to confirm S3 role ARN + test object (owner: Elena) — due 08/23
2) Redwood infra to provide temporary ingress keys and a smoke test (owner: Ben) — due 08/24
3) Security questionnaire: quick note on data retention policy (owner: Karthik) — due 08/25

Can you confirm the dates and whether we should push the evaluation window if ingestion slips further?

Thanks,
Ben

[Attachment: finera_map_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet)]
[Attachment: eval_acceptance_matrix.pdf (pdf)]

Cc: Finera POC Team <poc-team@finera.io>
Date: 2027-08-21T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP checkpoint: Week-of Aug 21 — POC Milestone tracker & acceptance binder

Ben —

Thanks for the tracker. Quick responses inline:

- We found the S3 role issue yesterday; ops are pushing the corrected policy this morning. I expect the test object to be available by EOD 08/22.
- Regarding latency: 250ms 95th is tight for our gateway currently. Can we set a staged acceptance: 250ms target for the final run, but allow a 350ms interim threshold for the POC so we can iterate on kernel/quantization? We will need to show improvement between POC baseline and final run.
- Tokenizer parity: Samir from our NLP team will supply a token sample set today.

We agree to the proposed owners but would like Redwood to own the interim latency measurement (so it's apples-to-apples).

Elena

On the attachments: we'll review and add comments directly to the tracker on Drive once you grant access.

title: Portfolio health dossier + renewal risk register (QBR prep)

Subject: Re: Portfolio health dossier + renewal risk register (QBR prep)

Ben — quick findings on the token anomaly:

- Root cause: a customer-side batch job changed prompt templates mid-July, increasing average token length ~18%. No model-side regression detected.

title: Post-call: Nimbus Health — inference fit, recommended models & benchmark links
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Lin <sarah@nimbushealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-08-21 13:05:00 -0000
Subject: Post-call: Nimbus Health — inference fit, recommended models & benchmark links

Hi Sarah,

Great to meet you and Mark on the call — thanks for walking through the EMR summarization and triage use cases. Quick recap and a recommended next set of items so our teams can scope an eval: 

Key needs you called out:
- Low-latency single-turn API for clinician-facing summaries (target p95 <= 350ms).
- High throughput nightly batch processing for overnight ingestion (throughput goal ~5000 docs/hr).
- Strong hallucination/accuracy requirements on medication lists and allergies.

Immediate suggestions (what we can deliver for a focused eval):
1) Recommended model variants to test (Redwood-verified profiles attached):
   - small-latency: rwdw-fast-7b-q3 -> best p50/p95 SLO for <250ms median with continuous batching off (good for real-time).
   - balanced: rwdw-stable-13b-q4 -> stronger factuality tradeoff, p95 ~350-420ms depending on token length.
   - throughput batch: rwdw-batch-13b-fp16 -> optimized for KV cache reuse and sustained TPS in batch jobs.

2) Benchmark pack & prompt set: Attached: redwood_verified_profiles.csv and nimbus_prompt_set_v1.txt. I also uploaded the raw run CSV and latency plots to the Drive link below.

3) Quality checks we suggest for the eval: medication extraction F1 test set (we can seed one), hallucination-focused prompts, and a small prompt perturbation suite to detect regressions across model versions.

Next steps:
- If you agree, we’ll spin up a 2-week evaluation using the three model configs above. Redwood will provide the perf pack + test harness; Nimbus to provide 200 sample cases and the medication gold labels.
- Tentative timeline: spin-up within 2 business days after NDA + data upload.

Links: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-nimbus-benchmarks (bench CSVs + latency plots)
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/4932

Attachments: redwood_verified_profiles.csv, nimbus_prompt_set_v1.txt, nimbus_perf_summary_20270821.pdf

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben@redwoodinference.com | (415) 555-0184




From: Sarah Lin <sarah@nimbushealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mark Zhou <mark.zhou@nimbushealth.com>
Date: 2027-08-21 13:42:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Post-call: Nimbus Health — inference fit, recommended models & benchmark links

Ben — thanks for the clear recap. Helpful. A few quick clarifications from our side: 

- p95 target of 350ms is for the clinician UI (single-turn) — we can tolerate higher in the batch path.
- For the gold labels, we can provide a 200-case set next week; is CSV format fine (patient_id, note_text, meds_list)?
- Can you confirm whether the rwdw-fast-7b-q3 profile uses prefix caching? That will affect latency in our environment.

We’re good to proceed with the 2-week eval once the legal/NDA is done. I looped Mark who will coordinate the sample data.

Thanks,
Sarah
Sr. Product Manager, Nimbus Health
sarah@nimbushealth.com

On Mon, Aug 21, 2027 at 13:05 Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Great to meet you and Mark on the call — thanks for walking through the EMR summarization and triage use cases. Quick recap and a recommended next set of items so our teams can scope an eval: ...




From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sarah Lin <sarah@nimbushealth.com>, Mark Zhou <mark.zhou@nimbushealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-08-21 14:18:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Post-call: Nimbus Health — inference fit, recommended models & benchmark links

Sarah — quick answers inline and next actions.

- CSV format for the gold labels is perfect. A sample record structure in the Drive folder under \"data-schema.md\".
- rwdw-fast-7b-q3: by default Redwood enables prefix KV caching for session-parallel flows, but for the clinician UI we can disable long-lived cache to keep p95 low; I’ve flagged both behaviors in the attached perf summary.

Laura / Rishi — can you pull the attached verified profiles and call out any config notes that Nimbus should be aware of for a VPC eval (quantization, memory footprint, required GPU type)? Rishi: can you also add a quick note on expected TPS for the batch pipeline with rwdw-batch-13b-fp16 based on the 5000 docs/hr ask?

Sarah / Mark — I’ll schedule a short tech-ops sync after NDA + sample data drop.

Ben

On Mon, Aug 21, 2027 at 13:42 Sarah Lin <sarah@nimbushealth.com> wrote:
> Ben — thanks for the clear recap. Helpful. A few quick clarifications from our side: ...




From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>, Sarah Lin <sarah@nimbushealth.com>, Mark Zhou <mark.zhou@nimbushealth.com>
Date: 2027-08-21 15:05:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Post-call: Nimbus Health — inference fit, recommended models & benchmark links

Looping in the technical detail asked for — quick notes and expected numbers based on our internal runs (attached CSV in Drive):

- rwdw-fast-7b-q3 (real-time single-turn):
  - Typical p50: ~95ms (1-2 tokens prompt), p95: ~220-260ms. Measured on a T4-equivalent instance with optimized kernel path and disabled long prefix caching.
  - Memory: ~6.2GB GPU RAM in q3 quantization; recommend a single 16GB GPU for headroom on concurrency.

- rwdw-stable-13b-q4 (balanced accuracy):
  - p50: ~180ms, p95: ~380-460ms depending on prompt length/response tokens.
  - Factuality: shows ~6% improvement on our medication extraction golden set versus the fast-7b profile in internal evals.

- rwdw-batch-13b-fp16 (throughput):
  - Sustained throughput: ~650-750 TPS (tokens/sec) under continuous batching; for your 5000 docs/hr target (avg doc ~600 tokens), this maps to ~7200-9000 tokens/sec sustained across a small pool — roughly 2x headroom with a 2-GPU pool configured for batch worker roles.

Caveats / regression concerns:
- Prompt perturbation increases hallucination cases in smaller models; we recommend the perturbation suite in nimbus_prompt_set_v1.txt be run at the start and end of the eval.
- Keep an eye on p95 spikes when concurrency increases — our runtime provides auto-routing fallback to the balanced variant to protect the UI SLA.

I’ll attach a short runbook in the Drive (under operations/) for the VPC eval — includes recommended GPU SKUs, batch worker sizes, and config flags to toggle caching/batching.

-Rishi
Principal Infra Engineer | Redwood Inference
rishi@redwoodinference.com



From: Sarah Lin <sarah@nimbushealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-08-21 16:42:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Post-call: Nimbus Health — inference fit, recommended models & benchmark links

Thanks all — this is exactly what we needed. I’ll route the NDA to legal today and Mark will drop the 200-sample CSV into the Drive folder by EOD tomorrow.

One ask from our infra team: once the sample data is in, can Redwood run the fast-7b and stable-13b profiles against our sample set and share the output JSON and a short diff showing medication-list mismatches? That will help our clinical reviewers triage fail cases quickly.

Sarah

title: Re: Celestia re-bid — playbook, benchmark plan, and capacity offer
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Celestia re-bid — urgent: benchmark plan and playbook

Team — Celestia (Jared/Procurement) surfaced a formal re-bid window this morning. They said a competitor has offered a short pilot and Celestia wants updated throughput/latency proof and a term-level response by end of week. Key asks from our side: quick benchmark, a canary pilot offer (dedicated pool), and a revised term sheet that protects revenue while addressing their TCO ask.

Quick summary of the requested targets from procurement call:
- Sustained traffic: 4–5M inference requests / day (mixed short/long sequences)
- 95th pct latency target: <200ms for chat flows
- Cost target: come in competitive vs their incumbent (they referenced 20% lower unit rate)

Immediate asks:
1) Marissa — lead the commercial response and advise on concession ceiling.
2) Naomi — can you run a fast cluster benchmark with the Celestia sample workload (I'll attach test plan)? Need results by EOD Wed.
3) Marcus — pre-build a dedicated capacity offer (GPU sizing + autoscale policy) and a fallback model variant (quantized) we can commit to during pilot.

Attached: rebid_test_plan.xlsx (traffic profile + sample payloads). I will coordinate the external ask.

Thanks — Aditya
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Attachment: rebid_test_plan.xlsx (Excel, 34KB)

From: Celestia Procurement <procurement@celestia.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Elaine Park <elaine@celestia.ai>, Jared Nunez <jared@celestia.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 11:03:22 -0700
Subject: Re: Celestia re-bid — RFP and timeline

Hi Aditya —

Thanks for flagging. Attaching the formal RFP and our target timeline (responses due Friday COB). A few clarifications from procurement/engineering:
- We need an end-to-end pilot that shows production-like telemetry (trace + token-level latency) against our sample dataset.
- Target sustained throughput: 5M req/day with occasional bursts to 8M; 95th <= 200ms for typical chat payloads (turns <= 1k tokens).
- Provide committed pricing for a 12‑month renewal with an uplift option at month 6 (capacity-based).

We're also evaluating PureCloud (unrelated name) who offered a 2-week trial and lower headline unit price. To remain competitive we need: 1) a clear pilot plan; 2) benchmark evidence; 3) a term sheet that includes a limited discount for committed capacity.

Attached: RFP_Q3-2027.pdf

—Jared Nunez
Head of Procurement, Celestia AI
procurement@celestia.ai
Attachment: RFP_Q3-2027.pdf (PDF, 120KB)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Jared Nunez <jared@celestia.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 12:47:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Celestia re-bid — playbook and pilot plan

Hi Jared / Elaine —

Appreciate the clarity and timeline. We can move fast on this: proposed immediate next steps below.

1) Short pilot (3 business days) — dedicated Redwood pool (pinned capacity) running Celestia sample traffic, with telemetry collection and dashboard access. We can provision a pre-warmed dedicated pool sized per your burst profile.
2) Benchmark window — Naomi will run a 48-hour sustained run with the exact payloads in the RFP (we'll share the raw logs + token-level p95/p99 latency).
3) Commercial proposal — Marissa & I will send committed-capacity pricing and a one-time onboarding credit.

Two asks for you to confirm:
- Can you share the sample corpus and an API key for the test harness?
- Is Celestia ok with our telemetry hooks (trace ids only, no customer PII) so we can produce comparable trace-level numbers?

I've attached the Celestia benchmark config we'll use. When you confirm I can kick off the provisioning with Marcus.

Thanks — Aditya

Attachment: celestia_benchmark_config.json (JSON, 8KB)

From: Elaine Park <elaine@celestia.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Jared Nunez <jared@celestia.ai>, Celestia Eng <eng-team@celestia.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 14:28:10 -0700
Subject: Re: Celestia re-bid — pilot details

Aditya —

Thanks. I shared the corpus and API key with your team (outbound SFTP link). Engineering ran a preliminary test and observed our baseline latency with Redwood on short sequences looks good, but for multi-turn long-context flows we saw higher p95 than expected in one sample trace (attached). We want the 48-hour sustained run you proposed and live telemetry to validate behavior under realistic mixing.

Also: procurement reiterated the price sensitivity — they quoted a 20% headline delta vs incumbent. If you can show comparable latency + a dedicated migration window (with rollback), we will escalate to execs with a view to keep you in the running.

On deck: we'll need the term sheet and pilot metrics by Thu EOD to get to exec review on Friday.

—Elaine
Director of Platform Engineering, Celestia AI
Attachment: celestia_sample_trace_excerpt.txt (TXT, 12KB)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Aug 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Celestia re-bid — internal pricing & save plan

Aditya — quick internal note before we send external terms.

Proposed commercial posture (recommended):
- Committed 12-month renewal with an upfront commit for a dedicated pool sized to 6M req/day (burst to 8M).
- Offer: 12% recurring discount off list for the 12-month commit + one-time onboarding credit equal to one month at committed rate.
- Conditional concession: additional 4% if Celestia signs within the re-bid window and agrees to a 6‑month capacity review clause (we can escalate final approval to RevOps).

Operational guarantees:
- SLA: 99.5% uptime for dedicated pool; p95 latency target documented in SLO appendix.
- Canary: 48-hour canary in their staging account with telemetry sharing and an agreed rollback plan.

Naomi — prioritize the 48-hour run and include p50/p95/p99 + throughput breakdowns by sequence length. Marcus — provide GPU SKU options and autoscale policy text and costs.

If we can deliver the benchmark numbers that match their p95 target and show a controlled migration path, this posture keeps us competitive without over-indexing on price.

Marissa Cole
Director of Strategic Accounts, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

Attachment: internal_pricing_notes.txt (TXT, 3KB)

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Jared Nunez <jared@celestia.ai>, Elaine Park <elaine@celestia.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Aug 2027 16:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Celestia re-bid — formal pilot offer and terms (D-98144)

Jared / Elaine —

Thanks for working through this quickly. Attached are: (1) benchmark_results_summary.pdf with the 48-hour sustained run data, (2) proposed_terms_v3.docx with the committed-capacity offer, and (3) a DocuSign link for the pilot agreement.

Headline summary:
- 48-hour sustained run: committed dedicated pool sized to 6M req/day; observed p95 = 185ms on mixed workload (detailed breakdown in PDF). p99 spikes were tied to long-context reads; we included a tuned KV-cache profile that reduced p99 by 28% in mixed tests.
- Commercial: 12% recurring discount for a 12-month committed pool; one-month onboarding credit applied; additional 4% conditional if signed by Friday (as Marissa outlined).
- Pilot: 3-day canary in your staging account with telemetry sharing and rollback stub. We will provide a post-pilot ops runbook and migration checklist.

Next steps:
1) If this aligns, please sign the pilot agreement via DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/templates/CEL-D-98144
2) Confirm SFTP payload is correct so Naomi can publish raw logs to your folder.
3) Schedule an exec touchpoint if you want us to present the benchmark to leadership — Marissa and I are available Fri morning.

We want to keep Celestia in the running and believe this is a low-risk approach to validate performance and value.

Regards —
Aditya Rao
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: benchmark_results_summary.pdf (PDF, 42KB); proposed_terms_v3.docx (DOCX, 28KB)

- Provide committed pricing for a 12‑month renewal with an uplift option at month 6 (capacity-based).

We're also evaluating PureCloud (unrelated name) who offered a 2-week trial and lower headline unit price. To remain competitive we need: 1) a clear pilot plan; 2) benchmark evidence; 3) a term sheet that includes a limited discount for committed capacity.

Attached: RFP_Q3-2027.pdf

—Jared Nunez

procurement@celestia.ai

title: OrbitAI — Dedicated capacity intake + SLA checklist
preview:
From: Emma Liu <emma.liu@orbitai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@orbitai.com
Date: Tue, 10 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity intake — initial demand profile and timelines

Hi Ben / Karthik,

Thanks for the meeting yesterday. Per our procurement process we've consolidated the initial demand profile and a focused set of RFP questions into RFP_Questions_v3.pdf (attached). High level: OrbitAI expects to move production inference for our Conversational Assistant (multi-turn, 8k context) and an embeddings pipeline for search into a dedicated pool.

Quick asks so we can finalize internal approvals this week: 
- Can you confirm a recommended GPU family mix for steady-state (target 2,000 token/sec across requests) vs peak (burst 5x) for latency-sensitive chat?
- Tail p95 target: 250ms for chat generation (end-to-end, model only) — is that realistic for dedicated?
- What's the typical lead time for Reserved Dedicated pools (30–100 GPUs)?

We attached a simple demand workbook but happy to receive a concise checklist / acceptance criteria you need from us (SLA questions, security evidence, expected traffic windows).

Attaching OrbitAI_DemandProfile.xlsx.

Appreciate the quick turnaround — we need to submit the procurement packet by EOD Friday.

Cheers,
Emma
Emma Liu
Head of Platform Engineering | OrbitAI
emma.liu@orbitai.com
[Attachment: OrbitAI_DemandProfile.xlsx (spreadsheet, 120KB)]

Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, procurement@orbitai.com
Date: Tue, 10 Aug 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity intake — initial demand profile and timelines

Emma — thanks for the packet and the clear asks. Looping Naomi (sales lead) and Karthik (inference architect) to provide the technical sizing + SLA checklist.

Top-level reply now: short answer is yes — dedicated is a good fit for your p95 target if we size for steady-state with headroom and enable burst lanes. We'll draft a one-page "intake + SLA checklist" for procurement and a separate technical worksheet with suggested GPU mix and burst policy.

Deliverables and timing: 
- Today EOD: intake + SLA checklist (one page) for your procurement packet.
- Wed: technical worksheet with steady-state and burst slot counts (including quantized model options).
- Fri: final numbers and a short slide for your procurement review if needed.

Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
[Attachment: none]

On Tue, Aug 10, 2027 at 09:12 AM Emma Liu <emma.liu@orbitai.com> wrote:
> (see above)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Emma Liu <emma.liu@orbitai.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, procurement@orbitai.com
Date: Wed, 11 Aug 2027 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity intake — initial demand profile and timelines

Emma — thanks for the workbook. I pulled the high-level constraints and sketched a conservative sizing plan. Main assumptions (please confirm):
- Conversational assistant workload: average generation length 140 tokens, median request tokens 220 (including context).
- 8k context requirement for multi-turn (mostly retrieval-augmented chunks, 6–8k active).
- Embeddings: batched offline/nearline jobs (lower latency constraint).

Recommendations summary:
1) GPU family mix (steady-state + burst):
   - Primary: H100 (80 GB) for chat model variants (LLM-13B to 70B non-quantized / quantized paths).
   - Secondary: A10G / L40s for embeddings and non-latency-critical rerank jobs to reduce cost.
   - For cost-sensitive paths, we can run int8/4-bit quantized variants on H100 or H800-equivalent but expect ~10–20% quality delta depending on model.

2) Throughput & slotting (baseline targets):
   - Steady-state: 2,000 token/sec across all chat requests (model-side throughput).
   - Tail SLO: p95 model-only < 250 ms for 1-pass generation (with continuous batching disabled for low tail).
   - Slot calculation (rough): each H100 can sustain ~45 concurrent short-chat streams at your token lengths with optimized kernels and KV cache reuse. That yields ~90–120k tokens/sec per 8-node H100 pod in our measurements for similar workloads.

3) Reservation sizing (initial commit):
   - Recommendation A (performance-first): Reserve 48 x H100 (baseline) + 16 x A10G for embeddings. This gives steady-state headroom and supports 5x burst if burst lanes are enabled (burst fills from on-demand mixed pool).
   - Recommendation B (cost-balanced): Reserve 32 x H100 + 24 x A10G and plan a burst policy that allows up to 1.5–2x transient capacity via shared burst lanes.

4) Tail/burst strategy:
   - Configure dedicated burst queue with warm H100 slots (cold-to-warm < 60s) and automatic model-variant fallback (quantized variant) for non-critical generations.

5) Lead times:
   - Typical lead time for 30–100 GPUs in Dedicated: 4–8 weeks for provisioning and validation, quicker (2–3 weeks) if you accept mixed-region/partner capacity.

I attached a small worksheet with the slot math and assumptions (RDW-SLA-Checklist-2027.xlsx). If you can confirm expected percent of requests that must hit strict p95 vs best-effort (e.g., 70% strict), I can re-run the mix calculation and give exact slot counts.

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Inference Architect | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com
[Attachment: RDW-SLA-Checklist-2027.xlsx (spreadsheet, 85KB)]

From: Emma Liu <emma.liu@orbitai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@orbitai.com, Carlos Mendes <carlos.m@orbitai.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Aug 2027 10:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity intake — initial demand profile and timelines

Thanks Karthik — this is very helpful. A couple of clarifications from OrbitAI side: 
- We need p95 strict on the public-facing chat for at least 80% of requests during business hours (08:00–20:00 PT). The other 20% can be best-effort.
- Peak factor: we've seen 3.8x peak-to-average during launches; to be safe we'd model 4.5x for initial procurement.
- Lead time constraint: procurement cannot accept >6 weeks for start of billing.

Request: can you produce a short one-page SLA checklist (items we must include for procurement to verify: uptime, p95 measuring methodology, eviction/fallback behavior, data residency, audit logs, support response times)? Ideally delivered today so we can attach to the packet.

Also, if you can mark which of the two recommendations (A/B) better satisfies our 4.5x peak while keeping lead time <=6 weeks, that helps our procurement approver.

Appreciate it — Emma

On Wed, Aug 11, 2027 at 08:22 AM Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com> wrote:
> (see above)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Emma Liu <emma.liu@orbitai.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, procurement@orbitai.com
Date: Wed, 18 Aug 2027 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity intake — initial demand profile and timelines

Emma — attaching the one-page intake + SLA checklist (embedded below) and confirming answers to your clarifications. Short version: Recommendation A (48x H100 + 16x A10G) meets 4.5x peak with burst lanes and keeps expected provisioning inside 4–6 weeks assuming we accept mixed-partner staging. Recommendation B reduces cost but will require more aggressive burst/eviction policies and carries higher risk for strict p95 during launch spikes.

--- BEGIN: Intake + SLA checklist (one page) ---
Required items for procurement packet (OrbitAI) — Redwood responses / notes:

1) Uptime SLA: 99.9% monthly availability for Dedicated pool (model-serving plane). See SLA matrix doc link.
2) Performance SLO measurement: p95 model-only latency measured at the request ingress to model egress (token generation complete), sampled at 1-minute windows and aggregated to hourly p95. Redwood will provide per-route p95 metrics and raw traces for 30 days by default (longer retention as add-on).

3) Tail behavior / eviction policy: Redwood supports warm burst lanes and automatic fallback to compatible quantized model variants. Eviction only occurs after configurable idle thresholds; instances are not preempted without a prioritized policy. We will provide a documented fallback map per model family during onboarding.

4) Data residency & isolation: Dedicated pools deployed in region (us-west-2) with VPC peering options and customer-controlled keys (KMS). No customer data stored in multi-tenant logs beyond aggregated telemetry.

5) Audit & compliance artifacts: SOC 2 Type II reports, access logs, and per-request audit trails available upon contract signature. Evidence delivery timeline: 2–4 business days for standard artifacts.

6) Support & escalation: 24x7 critical support for Dedicated customers with 30-minute critical incident response for paid enterprise tier. Standard business-hour coverage included.

7) Provisioning lead time: 4–6 weeks expected for 30–100 GPUs depending on capacity availability and networking approvals.

8) Billing & overage policy: Reserved baseline billed monthly; burst usage billed at overage rate with daily caps (negotiable).

9) Acceptance criteria for go-live: per-route p95 for production routes <250ms for 80% of traffic during business hours for three consecutive days, successful warm failover test, and security review completed.

10) Attachments we need from OrbitAI: final request percentiles (p50/p95/p99), expected peak calendar windows, and any regulatory constraints on logging retention.

--- END: Intake + SLA checklist ---

Attached: RDW-SLA-Checklist-2027.xlsx with explicit slot math for Recommendation A & B and a one-slide summary (if you want a slide for procurement, I can produce one).

Let me know if you want a short call tomorrow to walk procurement through the items.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
[Attachment: RDW-SLA-Checklist-2027.xlsx (spreadsheet, 85KB)]

title: Consumption health review and scaled-term recommendation — Nebula Labs
preview:
From: Tara Singh <tara@nebulalabs.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Rajiv Menon <rajiv.menon@nebulalabs.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: Q3 - Request: usage health + renewal conversation

Hi Alex,

Quick note from Nebula: we've seen a sustained spike in generation volume the last 6 weeks tied to a new feature we launched. We're approaching our committed run-rate and want to make sure we aren't going to hit throttles mid-quarter. High-level context:~42M tokens in trailing 30 days (aggregate), 60% of requests include long contexts (~1.2k tokens), and latency-sensitive workflows are our priority.

Can you do a short health/usage review and recommend options for scaling (commit increase vs dedicated pools)? We'd also like to understand the unit-economics and what a 12-month uplift would look like (savings vs current on-demand).

Attached: nebula-q3-usage.csv (request-level counts).

Would love a 30-min sync this week if possible. Our ASCT is 10am PT on Wed or Thu.

Thanks,
Tara
Tara Singh
Head of Product, Nebula Labs


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Tara Singh <tara@nebulalabs.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Q3 - Request: usage health + renewal conversation

Hi Tara — thanks for the data, that helps a lot. I pulled together an initial health snapshot and a few quick observations; attaching nebula-q3-health-report.csv for your review.

High-level summary (trailing 30 days):
- Total generated tokens: ~42,500,000
- Median tokens / request: ~350; 95th percentile ~1,150 (matches your long-context note)
- P95 latency (hosted): 180ms for short contexts, up to 480ms for >1k contexts
- Error/retry rate: 0.2% (no spiky degradation seen)

title: Adaptive Pack — tier swap options & stepdown guardrails
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>
To: Eli Turner <eli.turner@courtyard.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Adaptive Pack — tier swap options & stepdown guardrails

Hi Eli —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our conversation I sketched three practical options for Courtyard’s move from Compact to Adaptive Pack with a fallback path if load dips below expected thresholds. Attached is a short PDF that summarizes the tradeoffs; calling out the parts most relevant to you below so it’s quick to scan.

Quick TL;DR:
- Option A (Gradual swap): 3-month ramp to full Adaptive Pack, prorated credits if throughput drops >30% month-over-month. Lowest up-front commitment.
- Option B (Pilot commit): 6-month pilot with guaranteed per-token floor and a mid-term pricing review; includes 2 pilot seats for free; best for validating tail latency at scale.
- Option C (Reserved throughput): annual reserved capacity (lowest unit cost) + stepdown credit ladder on downgrades.

Two clarifying questions for procurement/legal before we lock language:
1) Does Courtyard need an explicit escape hatch for seat-level reductions (we proposed a 45-day notice window)?
2) Which percentile (95th vs. 99th) should we size the burst protection for — you mentioned 95th in the call but procurement asked about worst-case.

Next steps I suggest: pick Option A or B so we can attach the amendment and get procurement looking at the DocuSign. If useful, Ben can send a cost-delta sheet with line-item economics across your expected monthly sequence lengths.

Attachments: Adaptive_Pack_Options_v1.pdf (summary + decision grid)

Thanks —
Camila Reyes
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila_reyes@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0137

title: Routing & liability: draft partner-paper fallback policy for marketplace resales
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Maya Patel <maya@cloudseller.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Routing & liability: draft partner-paper fallback policy for marketplace resales

Maya / team —

Thanks again for the time today. Attached is the first pass at the partner paper that maps Redwood order-forms to CloudSeller resale flows. Two things we want to resolve before legal sign-off:

1) Fallback routing / model switch behavior when CloudSeller marketplace capacity is constrained (we need explicit pass-through exceptions and a clear SLA fallback chain).
2) Billing ownership and invoicing liability for resold seats (we proposed a reseller-invoice flow but want to capture audit triggers & evidence retention).

I've highlighted the clauses in the PDF (see attachment). Priyanka from Hall & Co is copied in case legal needs to weigh on the indemnity and revenue allocation language.

As next steps:
- Maya, can you confirm CloudSeller's required redline to the pass-through clause by EOD Monday?
- Ben/Laura: can you confirm whether we can accept the proposed escrow/audit language or if Ops needs additional telemetry for invoices?

Thanks — short meeting or quick call tomorrow could speed this.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Sr. Partner Lead, Redwood Inference
Redwood Inference

Attachments: CloudSeller_PartnerPaper_redlines_20270814.pdf; OrderForm_Attachment_BillingMatrix.xlsx


From: Priyanka Singh <psingh@hallandco.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Maya Patel <maya@cloudseller.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Aug 2027 11:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Routing & liability: draft partner-paper fallback policy for marketplace resales

Camila / Maya —

Quick read-through attached (redline comments inline). High-level observations:

- The pass-through carve-out as written is ambiguous about which party bears refunds if CloudSeller issues a credit post-fulfillment. Recommend adding a short waterfall: (1) CloudSeller issues credit to end-customer; (2) CloudSeller requests reimbursement from Redwood within 30 days; (3) if reprovisioning evidence is missing, an escrow holdback applies.

- Indemnity: suggest limiting Redwood's exposure when the issue is solely caused by CloudSeller marketplace policies or provider outages; propose a tiered liability cap tied to the affected invoice run.

- Audit scope: draft limits auditors to review only sales ledger rows related to resold orders; require reasonable notice and redaction of PII.

I flagged specific language and added an alternative clause for the invoice-hit path. If you want, I can produce a cleaned version that is operations-friendly (short bullets) for internal Ops review.

— Priyanka
Hall & Co. LLP (external counsel)

Attachment: HallCo_PartnerPaper_comments_20270814.pdf


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Priyanka Singh <psingh@hallandco.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 08:31:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Routing & liability: draft partner-paper fallback policy for marketplace resales

Camila / team —

Thanks Priyanka — these are helpful. From an ops/billing perspective:

- The waterfall approach works, but we need explicit ledger event ids and a 2-step evidence handoff: (1) CloudSeller posts the credit ID + reason; (2) Redwood verifies provisioning logs for the subscription id within 14 days. Without the event ids, recon will be blocked and finance will push chargebacks back to Redwood.

- For escrow: Ops prefers a capped holdback (e.g., 5% of the disputed invoice run) rather than an open-ended escrow. That keeps bookkeeping predictable.

- Can we add a clause that CloudSeller will include our internal invoice-reference or order-form id on any marketplace-issued credit? That's the minimal change that prevents seven-figure reconciliation errors.

I'll attach a short spreadsheet mapping the recommended ledger columns to the order-form fields.

Ben
Director, Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachment: OrderForm_Attachment_BillingMatrix.xlsx


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Maya Patel <maya@cloudseller.io>
Date: Mon, 16 Aug 2027 12:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Routing & liability: draft partner-paper fallback policy for marketplace resales

Team —

Couple operational clarifications from procurement/legal intake: 

- Payment terms: CloudSeller's standard is 45 days net to reseller; Redwood prefers 30 days. We should propose a compromise: 30 days for direct-sold orders, 45 for resold with pass-through evidence.

- Rollout phasing: suggest pilot the pass-through flow on one region (us-west) for 90 days and collect three reconciliation cycles before broad rollout. That reduces audit surface and gives us telemetry on failed reconciliations.

- For the fallback routing (when marketplace model unavailable), we must include a short SLA-triggered flow: automatic failover to Redwood-hosted ordering with customer notice and a 'no-surprise' price alignment clause. This prevents silent substitution.

I'll add those two bullets into the tracked redline and push a version to the shared link.

Karthik
Head of Procurement, Redwood Inference

Cc: Priyanka Singh <psingh@hallandco.com>

title: Termination-for-Convenience notice window & evidence request
preview:
From: Alice Nguyen <procurement@stellaris.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: legal@stellaris.io
Date: Sat, 14 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Questions on TfC notice window + auto-renew behavior

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the draft MSA and order form. Our legal and procurement teams reviewed the redline and had two substantive asks that we wanted to flag before we go into a scoped redline call:

1) Termination for Convenience (Section 6.2) — your draft says 90 days' written notice. We typically require 60 days in our standard contracts; would Redwood consider a 60-day window instead of 90? If not, can you confirm whether the 90-day window is negotiable for pilots/subscriptions under $100k TCV?

2) Auto-renew alignment — the order form's auto-renew paragraph references a 30-day notice to opt out prior to renewal. We'd like confirmation on timing for invoice/proration and whether customer can terminate during the renewal month without a fee.

Separately, our security team would like evidence that we can export audit logs for the 60/90-day period in the event of termination (timeline and retention). I've attached our redline and a sample audit request template.

Can we get a written position on both items before our legal call next Tuesday?

Attachments: Stellaris_MSA_Redlines_v2.docx, audit_log_template.pdf

Appreciate it —
Alice Nguyen
Senior Procurement Manager
Stellaris Systems
procurement@stellaris.io




From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alice Nguyen <procurement@stellaris.io>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 14 Aug 2027 12:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on TfC notice window + auto-renew behavior

Alice — thanks for the clear note and the attachments. We're happy to discuss live but wanted to put a proposed written position on the table ahead of the call so legal/procurement have something to react to.

Quick summary of our suggested approach:
- Termination for Convenience: Redwood can be flexible. For pilots and orders under $100k TCV we can accept a 60-day notice. For larger commitments we request 90 days unless the customer accepts a 45-day transition payment equal to 25% of remaining monthly committed spend.
- Auto-renew: The standard order form requires a 30-day written opt-out prior to the renewal effective date. If customer terminates during the renewal month (after the renewal date) standard billing applies for that billing cycle; we can consider prorated refunds on a case-by-case basis for hosted plans but not for reserved/dedicated capacity.
- Audit logs / exportability: We can provide exports for the 60/90-day window and have an audit retention policy of 12 months by default; for longer retention we can arrange a short-term export (CSV/ndjson) as part of offboarding.

I've attached a short redline response we plan to send to your legal team. Laura — can you take the legal redline on the 90 -> 60 exception for pilot sizing and draft the amendment language? Marissa — can you confirm ops feasibility on the export deliverable and estimated lead time?

Attachments: Redline_Response_Draft.docx

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Head of Sales Operations
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0183



From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 15 Aug 2027 10:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Questions on TfC notice window + auto-renew behavior

Ben / Marissa — reviewing Ben's suggested position. My legal take and suggested amendment language below:

1) TfC notice window: Accept 60 days for pilot and subscription orders with Total Contract Value (TCV) < $100,000. For larger orders, keep 90 days but add optional short-form transition fee: customer may elect a 45-day payment equal to the average monthly committed spend over the preceding 3 months (capped at 25% of remaining contract value).

Proposed contract language (high-level, for order form addendum):
"For Order Forms with TCV less than $100,000, the Parties agree that the termination-for-convenience notice period shall be sixty (60) days. For Order Forms with TCV greater than or equal to $100,000, the notice period shall be ninety (90) days, provided that Customer may instead elect to pay a transition payment equal to the lesser of (i) 25% of remaining contract value or (ii) forty-five (45) days of average monthly committed spend, as measured over the preceding three (3) months."

2) Auto-renew mechanics: Agree with Ben's baseline. Suggest we add explicit language that termination during the renewal month does not relieve Customer of payment for the then-current billing period. For hosted plans, include a provision that Redwood may, at its discretion, issue prorated refunds for partial-month service upon documented goodwill requests; for Dedicated capacity, no prorations.

3) Audit logs/exports: We should commit to providing an export of customer audit logs covering the prior 12 months on reasonable request and within 10 business days following a termination effective date. For customers who require ongoing longer retention at contract signing, include an addendum fee. Ops will need to confirm the exact export format and bandwidth constraints.

I'll incorporate the proposed language into Redline_Response_Draft.docx and flag the sections requiring finance signoff (the transition payment cap).

title: Odin Labs — Dedicated GPU pool turn-up, netops constraints & SLA exceptions
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Eleanor Hayes <eleanor.hayes@odinlabs.ai>, Tim Cho <tim.cho@odinlabs.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Aug 2027 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Odin Labs — Dedicated GPU pool turn-up, proposed schedule and constraints

Hi Eleanor / Tim —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Per our discussion: Odin Labs is targeting a hard go-live for the core inference path on a dedicated GPU pool (multitenant by region) with autoscale and a small burst buffer for peak traffic. I attached a first-pass SOW and a short autoscale simulation CSV so we can align on sizing.

Key asks from our side to make the handoff smooth:
- Confirm preferred primary region (you mentioned us-west) and an ordered fallback region.
- Confirm IP ranges / NAT requirements for allowlist and whether you need a private peering option.
- Confirm max sustained RPS and peak 5m burst concurrency for the first 30 days.
- Identify a NetOps contact to provision ephemeral VPN / whitelist entries during the cutover.

Proposed timeline (high level):
- Aug 18: NetOps prep + allowlist exchange
- Aug 20–22: Staging turn-up (model deploy, perf smoke tests)
- Aug 24: Limited production traffic routing (canary)
- Aug 31: Full cutover and SLA handoff

If that looks reasonable, I’ll schedule a 30-minute kickoff with our SRE and your NetOps to exchange access and firewall details.

Attachments: Odin-Dedicated-SOW-draft.pdf, odn-autoscale-sim.csv

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0147



From: Tim Cho <tim.cho@odinlabs.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Eleanor Hayes <eleanor.hayes@odinlabs.ai>
Cc: procurement@odinlabs.ai
Date: Wed, 11 Aug 2027 11:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Odin Labs — Dedicated GPU pool turn-up, proposed schedule and constraints

Thanks Ben — this looks good. Quick clarifications from our side:

1) Region: primary = us-west-2 (we run other infra there). Fallback should be eu-west-1.
2) IPs: We can provide static NAT ranges but prefer a private VPC peering; need cost estimate for that.
3) Throughput: initial baseline is 120 RPS sustained (median payload 1.4KB). Peak observed during load tests is ~1,100 concurrent token-streaming connections; plan for a 2x burst buffer during the first 2 weeks.
4) Compliance: we need to see the KMS/HSM integration notes and an audit-log retention policy.

We have NetOps on standby — Eleanor is the technical owner, procurement will loop in for DocuSign. Can you confirm if the burst policy supports instant scale to additional nodes without dropping in-flight streams? That’s a hard requirement for us.

- Tim
Director, Platform Engineering — Odin Labs

To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>

- Estimate required vGPU count for 120 RPS sustained / 1,100 concurrent streaming, with a 2x burst margin. Use the sim CSV attached.
- Clarify peering vs static NAT cost delta and any IP allowlist automation we can provide.
- Prepare a short (1-page) note on KMS/HSM integration and audit retention for compliance.

Can Monica lead the ops checklist and Karthik/Naomi provide capacity estimates? I’ll shepherd the external scheduling and procurement.

Thanks —
Ben
